Reported Symptom: Right-side customer drawer always showed the same name (e.g. Lucio Bruno) regardless of which customer was clicked; phone/items were correct.
Root Cause: Name read via a find() over ALL items (first named item) instead of the clicked customer.
Fix: Name and phone now read from the clicked customer's own items.
How QA Verifies: Open the customer drawer for several different customers in Open Items; header name + phone must match the clicked row every time.

Reported Symptom: Listen/Transcript could play a DIFFERENT call than the item came from (wrong recording/transcript/summary).
Root Cause: When the item's own call id failed to load it silently fell back to the customer's most recent call and showed it as the source.
Fix: Fallback runs only when the item has no call id at all, and shows an amber banner that it may not be this item's call; an item with a real id that fails shows its evidence excerpt instead of an unrelated call.
How QA Verifies: Item with a real recording -> correct call, no banner. Call item with no linked call -> amber banner appears. Transcript/recording always belongs to the same customer.

Reported Symptom: Browse all conversations -> clicking a call did nothing, or showed the previous call's details.
Root Cause: Report fetch keyed off the item, not the clicked call, so a drilled call never loaded.
Fix: Drilling into a call now fetches that specific call's report.
How QA Verifies: Open a customer's call drawer -> Browse all conversations -> click a different call -> its recording/transcript/summary loads; Back and pick another -> it updates.

Reported Symptom: A sales item could resolve its call against service conversations (wrong context).
Root Cause: Conversation lookup defaulted to the global 'service' department instead of the item's own department.
Fix: Lookup now uses the item's own department.
How QA Verifies: On a sales item confirm resolved call/conversations are sales-side; repeat on a service item.

Reported Symptom: On busy rooftops an item could appear twice and inflate the Repeat callers count.
Root Cause: Sequential paged fetch had no dedup; a row shifting between pages was fetched twice.
Fix: Items de-duplicated by id across pages.
How QA Verifies: On a large multi-page backlog scroll the full queue: no duplicate rows; repeat-caller counts sane.

Reported Symptom: A customer who contacted once but generated several action items was labelled an N-time repeat caller.
Root Cause: Repeat count counted raw action items, not distinct conversations.
Fix: Now counts distinct conversations/calls per customer.
How QA Verifies: Customer whose multiple items came from one call/email -> badge x1; customer with separate calls -> true count.

Reported Symptom: (Hardening) Rare chance a panel keeps a previous item's/customer's state.
Fix: Both drawers remount per item/customer.
How QA Verifies: Rapidly open different customers/items in succession: no stale content carries over.

Reported Symptom: (Hardening) Searching a customer with no item in the current queue selected the wrong customer.
Root Cause: Search built candidates from the global customer map, not the scoped queue.
Fix: Search candidates now come from the items in the queue.
How QA Verifies: Type a name/phone in queue search; picking a result always selects that exact customer.

Reported Symptom: (Hardening) A customer's name/phone could degrade to 'Customer'/blank.
Root Cause: A later sparse duplicate record overwrote a good name/phone.
Fix: Merge-fill instead of overwrite.
How QA Verifies: Names/phones stay populated across refreshes and tab switches.

Reported Symptom: Resolved/Incorrect items disappeared after refresh or when switching Sales<->Service.
Fix: Resolved/Incorrect read from the DB (completed items with a console resolution).
How QA Verifies: Resolve an item -> refresh -> still under Resolved. Flag Incorrect -> refresh -> still there. Switch depts and back -> persists.

Reported Symptom: After resolving, the count reverted on refresh.
Root Cause: Pending total was computed from a partial page.
Fix: Pending fetch paginates the full backlog so resolved items are correctly excluded.
How QA Verifies: Resolve several items -> refresh -> counts stay reduced.

Reported Symptom: Sales/Service needed separate deep-links; department not in URL correctly.
Fix: serviceType is the canonical department URL param; toggle writes it back.
How QA Verifies: Load ?serviceType=service -> Service active. Toggle to Sales -> URL updates to serviceType=sales. Deep-link both.

Reported Symptom: Service tab showed Sales items and vice-versa.
Root Cause: Department derived from shared intent code.
Fix: Per-item department derived from the item's service_type.
How QA Verifies: In Service every item is service; in Sales every item is sales; no bleed.

Reported Symptom: Address bar didn't sync with the Sales/Service toggle inside the iframe.
Fix: postMessage bridge added; host must apply HOST-INTEGRATION.md to forward serviceType.
How QA Verifies: With the host patch applied, toggling the tab updates the parent URL. Depends on converse-ai host change.

Reported Symptom: 'No recording available' even when a recording existed.
Root Cause: Recording URL was relative; player rejected it.
Fix: Recording URL is absolute, routed through the same-origin proxy.
How QA Verifies: Open an item with a recording -> it plays.

Reported Symptom: SMS/chat action items not showing or mis-channelled.
Fix: SMS channel classification fixed; items appear under Channel = SMS with the thread.
How QA Verifies: Filter Channel = SMS -> SMS items appear with their message thread.

Reported Symptom: Queue not ordered by urgency.
Fix: Sort by longest SLA breach first (absolute overdue time).
How QA Verifies: Top of the queue is the most-overdue customer.

Reported Symptom: Couldn't search by phone number.
Fix: Phone search added (ignores formatting and +1).

Reported Symptom: Callbacks quick-filter missed items; Listen disabled despite a resolvable call.
Root Cause: Chip matched wrong intents; Listen gated on channel.
Fix: Callbacks chip matches all real callback intents; Listen enabled when a call is resolvable.
How QA Verifies: Callbacks chip returns all callback-type items; Listen enabled on items with a call.

Reported Symptom: 'At-risk' tagging/filter confusing (product call to remove).
Fix: Removed At-risk chip + filter.
How QA Verifies: No At-risk chip anywhere.

Reported Symptom: Manager/My-queue toggle to be removed.
Fix: Removed the scope toggle.
How QA Verifies: No Manager/My-queue toggle.

Reported Symptom: Resolver identity for auto-assign.
Fix: Resolver taken from session identity (userId/userEmail in URL).
How QA Verifies: Resolving an item records the acting user as owner.

Reported Symptom: customer_id can fall back to lead_id; two conversations from the same person under different lead ids may split into two customers.
Fix: Not changed - needs BE confirmation.
How QA Verifies: N/A - product/backend decision.

Reported Symptom: 'Repeat caller' is a same-session proxy (distinct conversations currently loaded), not a lifetime count.
Fix: Not changed - confirm acceptable or add a BE count.
How QA Verifies: N/A - product/backend decision.

Reported Symptom: Host<->iframe URL sync requires the converse-ai host to apply HOST-INTEGRATION.md.
Fix: Handover doc provided.
How QA Verifies: N/A - host team action.
